en.changeSummary: A severe thunderstorm is now underway at the observation site, with heavy rain, hail, and gusts to {q:gust:41} — conditions more serious than the rain forecast the prior report committed to. The morning block through {q:time:2026-04-21T17:00:00Z} has been upgraded to a severe storm.
en.closing: Tuesday brings a severe storm through the morning hours, with heavy rain, hail, and gusty winds giving way to rain continuing into the afternoon and evening. Highs Tuesday reach {q:temp_range:19:21}, with overnight lows near {q:temp_range:15:17}.

input.reasoning_trace:
Step 1 — TAF vs GFS: The TAF issuance (2026-04-21T11:38Z) is LATER than the GFS run (2026-04-21T06:00Z), so the TAF prevails for blocks within its validity window. The TAF shows showers/overcast through the period with a 30% probability of thunderstorms in the 19–23Z window — this supports ongoing precipitation but does not itself drive a severe upgrade.

Step 2 — METAR override: The 13:53Z observation (covering the 11:00–17:00Z block) reports Heavy Thunderstorm Rain Hail, winds 230° at 25 mph (≈22 kt) gusting 47 mph (≈41 kt), visibility 1.5 SM, and cumulonimbus at 2500 ft. This is a safety-critical observation: hail is present, winds are gusty and strong, and a severe thunderstorm is actively occurring. The 11:00–17:00Z block is overridden: precipPhenomenon → thunderstorm, severeFlag → true, precipExpectation → certain, windKt adjusted to reflect observed sustained winds (≈22 kt max, min 13 kt). The gust (41 kt) is placed only in the narrative {q:gust} token, NOT in windKt. The 17:00Z block is left as rain/overcast per TAF and GFS, with no severe signal for that period.

Step 3 — vs prior_snapshot: The prior committed forecast for the 11:00–17:00Z block was precipPhenomenon rain, severeFlag false. The new reconciled block is precipPhenomenon thunderstorm, severeFlag true — a safety-critical upgrade (hail observed, severe thunderstorm in progress). This is unambiguously news worth an unscheduled send regardless of the "observation only" changed_since_last_sent_report flag, because the observation shows weather ARRIVING (a severe storm with hail) that the prior forecast did not promise. The anti-reversal rule does not apply here — it prevents downgrading a prior severe call on observation only; it does not prevent upgrading when a severe event is actively observed. submit_reconciled_report is warranted.
